004 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.

006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with
007 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
008 *
009 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
010 *
011 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
013 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
014 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.commons.inject;
018
019import static java.util.stream.Collectors.*;
020import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.CollectionUtils.*;
021import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Shorts.*;
022
023import java.util.*;
024import java.util.function.*;
025
026import org.apache.juneau.commons.reflect.*;
027
028/**
029 * Spring-bean-like interface for looking up beans by type and name.
030 *
031 * <p>
032 * This interface provides a minimal contract for bean lookup operations, similar to Spring's
033 * <a class="doclink" href="https://docs.spring.io/spring-framework/docs/current/javadoc-api/org/springframework/beans/factory/BeanFactory.html">BeanFactory</a> interface.
034 *
035 * <p>
036 * Implementations of this interface can be backed by various sources:
037 * <ul>
038 *    <li>Spring's <a class="doclink" href="https://docs.spring.io/spring-framework/docs/current/javadoc-api/org/springframework/context/ApplicationContext.html">ApplicationContext</a>
039 *    <li>Custom in-memory stores
040 *    <li>Other dependency injection frameworks
041 * </ul>
042 *
043 * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
044 * <p class='bjava'>
045 *    BeanStore <jv>beanStore</jv> = ...;
046 *
047 *    <jc>// Get bean by type</jc>
048 *    Optional&lt;MyService&gt; <jv>service</jv> = <jv>beanStore</jv>.getBean(MyService.<jk>class</jk>);
049 *
050 *    <jc>// Get bean by name</jc>
051 *    Optional&lt;Object&gt; <jv>bean</jv> = <jv>beanStore</jv>.getBean(<js>"myBean"</js>);
052 *
053 *    <jc>// Get bean by type and name</jc>
054 *    Optional&lt;MyService&gt; <jv>namedService</jv> = <jv>beanStore</jv>.getBean(MyService.<jk>class</jk>, <js>"primary"</js>);
055 *
056 *    <jc>// Get all beans of a type</jc>
057 *    Map&lt;String, MyService&gt; <jv>allServices</jv> = <jv>beanStore</jv>.getBeansOfType(MyService.<jk>class</jk>);
058 * </p>
059 *
060 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
061 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://docs.spring.io/spring-framework/reference/core/beans.html">Spring Framework - The IoC Container</a>
062 * </ul>
063 */
064public interface BeanStore {
065
066   /**
067    * Returns a bean of the specified type.
068    *
069    * <p>
070    * If multiple beans of the specified type exist, the behavior is implementation-dependent.
071    * Typically, implementations will return the primary bean or throw an exception.
072    *
073    * @param <T> The bean type.
074    * @param beanType The bean type.
075    * @return The bean, or {@link Optional#empty()} if no bean of the specified type exists.
076    */
077   <T> Optional<T> getBean(Class<T> beanType);
078
079   /**
080    * Returns a bean of the specified type and name.
081    *
082    * @param <T> The bean type.
083    * @param beanType The bean type.
084    * @param name The bean name.  Can be <jk>null</jk>.
085    * @return The bean, or {@link Optional#empty()} if no bean of the specified type and name exists.
086    */
087   <T> Optional<T> getBean(Class<T> beanType, String name);
088
089   /**
090    * Returns all beans of the specified type, keyed by bean name.
091    *
092    * <p>
093    * The map keys are the bean names, and the values are the bean instances.
094    * If no beans of the specified type exist, returns an empty map.
095    *
096    * @param <T> The bean type.
097    * @param beanType The bean type.
098    * @return A map of bean names to bean instances.  Never <jk>null</jk>.
099    */
100   <T> Map<String, T> getBeansOfType(Class<T> beanType);
101
102   /**
103    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this store contains at least one bean of the specified type.
104    *
105    * @param beanType The bean type.
106    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this store contains at least one bean of the specified type.
107    */
108   boolean hasBean(Class<?> beanType);
109
110   /**
111    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this store contains a bean of the specified type and name.
112    *
113    * @param beanType The bean type.
114    * @param name The bean name.  Can be <jk>null</jk>.
115    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this store contains a bean of the specified type and name.
116    */
117   boolean hasBean(Class<?> beanType, String name);
118
119   /**
120    * Convenience shortcut for instantiating a bean of the specified type using this store.
121    *
122    * <p>
123    * Equivalent to:
124    * <p class='bjava'>
125    *    BeanInstantiator.<jsm>of</jsm>(<jv>type</jv>, <jk>this</jk>).run()
126    * </p>
127    *
128    * <p>
129    * Use this when you don't need any of {@link BeanInstantiator}'s configuration options
130    * (subtype, factory-method names, builder hooks, etc.).  For more control over instantiation,
131    * use {@link BeanInstantiator#of(Class, BeanStore)} directly.
132    *
133    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
134    * <p class='bjava'>
135    *    <jc>// Instantiate a bean using this store for parameter resolution</jc>
136    *    MyService <jv>service</jv> = <jv>beanStore</jv>.instantiate(MyService.<jk>class</jk>);
137    * </p>
138    *
139    * @param <T> The bean type.
140    * @param type The bean type to instantiate.
141    * @return The instantiated bean.
142    */
143   default <T> T instantiate(Class<T> type) {
144      return BeanInstantiator.of(type, this).run();
145   }
146
147   /**
148    * Returns the supplier for an unnamed bean of the specified type.
149    *
150    * <p>
151    * If no supplier is found in this store, searches the parent store recursively.
152    *
153    * @param <T> The bean type.
154    * @param beanType The bean type.
155    * @return The supplier, or {@link Optional#empty()} if no supplier of the specified type exists.
156    */
157   <T> Optional<Supplier<T>> getBeanSupplier(Class<T> beanType);
158
159   /**
160    * Returns the supplier for a named bean of the specified type.
161    *
162    * <p>
163    * If no supplier with the specified name is found in this store, searches the parent store recursively.
164    *
165    * @param <T> The bean type.
166    * @param beanType The bean type.
167    * @param name The bean name.  Can be <jk>null</jk>.
168    * @return The supplier, or {@link Optional#empty()} if no supplier of the specified type and name exists.
169    */
170   <T> Optional<Supplier<T>> getBeanSupplier(Class<T> beanType, String name);
171
172   /**
173    * Finds and invokes a factory method that produces a bean of type <c>beanType</c>.
174    *
175    * <p>
176    * Scans all public methods of the resource class identified by <c>onClassOrObject</c>:
177    * <ul>
178    *    <li>If <c>onClassOrObject</c> is a {@link Class}, only <jk>static</jk> methods are eligible and
179    *       the instance parameter passed to the invoked method is <jk>null</jk>.
180    *    <li>If <c>onClassOrObject</c> is any other object, both instance and static methods on its class are
181    *       eligible, and the object itself is passed as the receiver.
182    * </ul>
183    *
184    * <p>
185    * The first method satisfying all of the following is invoked:
186    * <ol>
187    *    <li>Not deprecated.
188    *    <li>Return type is assignment-compatible with <c>beanType</c>.
189    *    <li>Accepted by <c>filter</c> (if non-<jk>null</jk>; otherwise any method qualifies).
190    *    <li>All parameters can be resolved from this store plus any <c>extraBeans</c>.
191    * </ol>
192    *
193    * <p>
194    * The default implementation returns {@link Optional#empty()}.
195    * Override in concrete stores (see {@link BasicBeanStore}) to enable factory-method scanning.
196    *
197    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
198    * <p class='bjava'>
199    *    <jc>// Filter only</jc>
200    *    <jv>beanStore</jv>.createBeanFromMethod(CallLogger.<jk>class</jk>, <jv>resource</jv>,
201    *       RestContext::isBeanMethod)
202    *       .ifPresent(<jv>creator</jv>::impl);
203    *
204    *    <jc>// Filter + extra bean not yet in the store</jc>
205    *    <jv>beanStore</jv>.createBeanFromMethod(EncoderSet.<jk>class</jk>, <jv>resource</jv>,
206    *       RestContext::isBeanMethod, <jv>builder</jv>)
207    *       .ifPresent(<jv>x</jv> -&gt; <jv>builder</jv>.impl(<jv>x</jv>));
208    *
209    *    <jc>// No filter, no extra beans</jc>
210    *    <jv>beanStore</jv>.createBeanFromMethod(CallLogger.<jk>class</jk>, <jv>resource</jv>);
211    * </p>
212    *
213    * @param <T> The bean type.
214    * @param beanType The type of bean to create.  Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
215    * @param onClassOrObject The object instance or {@link Class} whose public methods are searched.
216    *    Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
217    * @param filter Optional predicate restricting which methods are eligible.  Can be <jk>null</jk>.
218    * @param extraBeans Optional bean instances visible to parameter resolution for this call only.
219    *    These are <em>not</em> registered in the store.
220    * @return The created bean wrapped in an {@link Optional}, or {@link Optional#empty()} if no matching
221    *    factory method was found.
222    * @throws BeanCreationException If a matching method was found but threw an exception during invocation.
223    * @see BeanInstantiator BeanInstantiator — for instantiating a bean from its own constructors, builders, or factory methods
224    */
225   default <T> Optional<T> createBeanFromMethod(Class<T> beanType, Object onClassOrObject, Predicate<MethodInfo> filter, Object... extraBeans) {
226      return oe();
227   }
228
229   /**
230    * Convenience overload of {@link #createBeanFromMethod(Class, Object, Predicate, Object...)} with no filter and no extra beans.
231    *
232    * @param <T> The bean type.
233    * @param beanType The type of bean to create.  Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
234    * @param onClassOrObject The object instance or {@link Class} whose public methods are searched.
235    *    Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
236    * @return The created bean wrapped in an {@link Optional}, or {@link Optional#empty()} if no matching
237    *    factory method was found.
238    * @throws BeanCreationException If a matching method was found but threw an exception during invocation.
239    */
240   default <T> Optional<T> createBeanFromMethod(Class<T> beanType, Object onClassOrObject) {
241      return createBeanFromMethod(beanType, onClassOrObject, null);
242   }
243
244   /**
245    * Returns the implementation class registered for the specified bean type, if any.
246    *
247    * <p>
248    * Type bindings let callers register a default implementation class to be instantiated when an
249    * actual bean of <c>beanType</c> is needed.  Bindings are inherited through the parent chain.
250    *
251    * <p>
252    * The default implementation returns {@link Optional#empty()} (no binding).  Override in concrete
253    * stores (see {@link BasicBeanStore}) to enable type binding.
254    *
255    * @param <T> The bean type.
256    * @param beanType The bean type whose implementation class is being looked up.
257    * @return The implementation class, or {@link Optional#empty()} if no binding exists.
258    */
259   default <T> Optional<Class<? extends T>> getBeanType(Class<T> beanType) {
260      return oe();
261   }
262
263   /**
264    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this store can satisfy every parameter of the specified executable.
265    *
266    * <p>
267    * Skips:
268    * <ul>
269    *    <li>The first parameter when it is an instance of <c>outer</c> (inner-class outer reference).
270    *    <li>Parameters typed as {@link Optional} (always considered satisfied).
271    * </ul>
272    *
273    * @param executable The constructor or method to inspect.
274    * @param outer The outer object to use for inner-class param resolution.  Can be <jk>null</jk>.
275    * @return <jk>true</jk> if every required parameter can be resolved from this store.
276    */
277   default boolean hasAllParams(ExecutableInfo executable, Object outer) {
278      for (var i = 0; i < executable.getParameterCount(); i++) {
279         var pi = executable.getParameter(i);
280         var pt = pi.getParameterType();
281         if ((i == 0 && nn(outer) && pt.isInstance(outer)) || pt.is(Optional.class))
282            continue;
283         var beanQualifier = pi.getResolvedQualifier();
284         var ptc = pt.inner();
285         if ((beanQualifier == null && ! hasBean(ptc)) || (nn(beanQualifier) && ! hasBean(ptc, beanQualifier)))
286            return false;
287      }
288      return true;
289   }
290
291   /**
292    * Resolves every parameter of the specified executable from this store.
293    *
294    * <p>
295    * For {@link Optional}-typed parameters the wrapped {@link Optional} is returned directly; for all
296    * others the bean instance (or <jk>null</jk> when missing) is returned.  See {@link #hasAllParams}
297    * for the full skip rules.
298    *
299    * @param executable The constructor or method to resolve parameters for.
300    * @param outer The outer object to use for inner-class param resolution.  Can be <jk>null</jk>.
301    * @return An array of resolved parameter values, one per parameter of <c>executable</c>.
302    */
303   default Object[] getParams(ExecutableInfo executable, Object outer) {
304      var o = new Object[executable.getParameterCount()];
305      for (var i = 0; i < executable.getParameterCount(); i++) {
306         var pi = executable.getParameter(i);
307         var pt = pi.getParameterType();
308         if (i == 0 && nn(outer) && pt.isInstance(outer)) {
309            o[i] = outer;
310         } else {
311            var beanQualifier = pi.getResolvedQualifier();
312            var ptc = pt.unwrap(Optional.class).inner();
313            var o2 = beanQualifier == null ? getBean(ptc) : getBean(ptc, beanQualifier);
314            o[i] = pt.is(Optional.class) ? o2 : o2.orElse(null);
315         }
316      }
317      return o;
318   }
319
320   /**
321    * Returns a comma-delimited list of parameter type names that cannot be resolved from this store.
322    *
323    * <p>
324    * Intended for diagnostic error messages — see {@link #hasAllParams} for the exact match rules.
325    * Skipped parameters (outer-instance match, {@link Optional}) never appear in the result.
326    *
327    * @param executable The constructor or method to inspect.
328    * @param outer The outer object to use for inner-class param resolution.  Can be <jk>null</jk>.
329    * @return A comma-delimited list of missing parameter type names, or <jk>null</jk> when none are missing.
330    */
331   default String getMissingParams(ExecutableInfo executable, Object outer) {
332      var params = executable.getParameters();
333      List<String> l = list();
334      for (var i = 0; i < params.size(); i++) {
335         var pi = params.get(i);
336         var pt = pi.getParameterType();
337         if ((i == 0 && nn(outer) && pt.isInstance(outer)) || pt.is(Optional.class))
338            continue;
339         var beanName = pi.getResolvedQualifier();
340         var ptc = pt.inner();
341         if (beanName == null && ! hasBean(ptc))
342            l.add(pt.getNameSimple());
343         if (nn(beanName) && ! hasBean(ptc, beanName))
344            l.add(pt.getNameSimple() + '@' + beanName);
345      }
346      return l.isEmpty() ? null : l.stream().sorted().collect(joining(","));
347   }
348}
349
